query: What are the top AI news headlines today? Include sources.
textResponse:
Here are a few of the **top AI headlines circulating today, Thursday, April 9, 2026**, based on recent reporting and current AI coverage I found:

- **OpenAI Foundation pledges $1 billion in grants** to support life sciences, health research, and work on AI’s social impacts, including jobs, the economy, and children’s mental health. AP reported the announcement on **March 24, 2026**. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/286c962e8da3e12e4a8310ddc1543a6d?utm_source=openai))

- **OpenAI’s Pentagon/defense work remains a major AI story in 2026.** TechCrunch’s March 13 roundup said OpenAI moved to allow its models to be deployed in classified settings after a dispute between Anthropic and the U.S. military over usage limits, making military AI policy one of the year’s biggest flashpoints. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/13/the-biggest-ai-stories-of-the-year-so-far/?utm_source=openai))

- **Google is expanding Gemini across Workspace apps.** TechCrunch’s current AI coverage page lists Google rolling out new Gemini capabilities to **Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Drive**, signaling continued competition around workplace AI assistants. ([m.techcrunch.com](https://m.techcrunch.com/?utm_source=openai))

- **Anthropic has launched a code-review product aimed at AI-generated software.** TechCrunch lists a new Anthropic tool focused on reviewing the growing volume of AI-written code, highlighting the shift from model releases to practical enterprise tooling. ([m.techcrunch.com](https://m.techcrunch.com/?utm_source=openai))

- **Meta is still pushing AI through consumer and social products.** TechCrunch’s recent coverage notes Meta acquired **Moltbook**, an AI-agent social network that had gone viral for fake posts, showing how AI social products are becoming a live platform issue. ([m.techcrunch.com](https://m.techcrunch.com/?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can also turn this into a cleaner **“Top 10 AI headlines today”** list with **one-line summaries and publisher links only from major outlets like Reuters, AP, The Verge, and TechCrunch**.
